name: nonzero_mask_empty_view
reason: A nonzero mask entry should mean at least one bit is represented in the view, so the view must not be empty

name: view_contains_negative
reason: View elements are a*64+b with a in [0,8) and b in [0,64), so no element should ever be negative

name: view_always_empty
reason: If the view is always empty regardless of mask contents, is_empty trivially always returns true and the abstraction is useless

name: all_ones_mask_is_empty
code:
proof fn phi_4_all_ones_mask_is_empty(cm: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm.mask[0] == !0usize,
        cm.mask[1] == !0usize,
        cm.mask[2] == !0usize,
        cm.mask[3] == !0usize,
        cm.mask[4] == !0usize,
        cm.mask[5] == !0usize,
        cm.mask[6] == !0usize,
        cm.mask[7] == !0usize,
    ensures
        cm@ == Set::<int>::empty(),
{
    lemma_is_bit_set();
    cm.lemma_view();
}
reason: A mask with every bit set in every field should have the fullest possible view, never empty

name: bit0_not_in_view
reason: If bit 0 of mask[0] is set, element 0*64+0=0 must appear in the view; its absence would break the bit-to-set mapping
